ali-france-dickson.summary: In November 2016, before entering Parliament, Ali France publicly urged then-PM Malcolm Turnbull to reconsider a proposed lifetime ban on asylum seekers who arrived by boat, writing an open letter and citing her surgeon, an Iraqi refugee who came to Australia by boat; she said genuine refugees 'should be given a chance.' Wikipedia characterises her as a critic of offshore detention. Her July 2025 first speech did not address immigration.

sources.title: This Woman Owes Her Quality Of Life To A Refugee Who Came Here By Boat
Amputee saved by refugee surgeon urges PM to rethink asylum seeker ban
Ali France

bob-katter-kennedy.summary: Bob Katter (KAP) called in February 2024 for Australia's migrant intake to be 'cut in half' and the student intake reduced by two-thirds, saying migration preference 'has to come from countries with democracy, egalitarian traditions, rule of law, Christian principles and industrial awards.' On asylum seekers he advocates turning back boats 'with, of course, fuel and food.' In January 2026 he reiterated a policy of 'No migration without assimilation.'

sources.title: Katter calls for immigration cuts after Ipswich fatal stabbing
Migration Legislation
Katter calls for 'No Migration without Assimilation'

anika-wells-lilley.summary: In response to the nationwide anti-immigration 'March for Australia' rallies of 31 August 2025, Wells condemned them as 'awful scenes' involving 'known racists,' saying 'we all have a responsibility to speak up and assure people from migrant communities ... that we stand with them and this isn't Australia.'

sources.title: Interview, Sky News Newsday
Thousands rally against immigration with neo-Nazis taking the spotlight

mehreen-faruqi-nsw.summary: As the Australian Greens' immigration and citizenship spokesperson, Faruqi advocates for refugees and asylum seekers and seeks an end to mandatory detention. In September 2025 she moved in the Senate to condemn anti-immigration marches as 'racist, white supremacist rallies' and accused politicians and media of normalising hate.

sources.title: Faruqi accuses Senate of 'normalising' and 'legitimising' hate after anti-immigration marches

sean-bell-nsw.summary: Bell named 'ending mass immigration' among his top Senate priorities, arguing that high immigration levels are driving housing unaffordability, overloading infrastructure and pushing up energy prices in NSW, and calling for a government that puts 'Australian families, industries, and values first.'

sources.title: Incoming One Nation senator vows to end mass migration

dave-sharma-nsw.summary: In his March 2024 Senate first speech, Sharma linked high immigration to housing pressure, saying 'the shortage of housing is being exacerbated by high immigration' and that 'until we are able to accelerate the pace of our home building, we need to reduce our immigration intake.'

sources.title: Sen. Dave Sharma (NSW-Lib) First Speech

david-shoebridge-nsw.summary: As the Greens spokesperson on immigration, Shoebridge opposes the Labor government's resettlement deal with Nauru, calling for it to be 'ripped up' and for 'an immediate pause on sending any further people to Nauru,' and has called for a royal commission into offshore processing.

sources.title: Calls for 'immediate pause' of 'secret' Nauru deal

larissa-waters-qld.summary: In a public statement on her official Facebook page (31 May 2024), Waters said 'we should be proud of multicultural and migrant communities, but the Labor and Liberal parties want to demonise migrants to distract from their own failings.' As Greens leader she has rejected cuts to overall migration numbers and the framing of migrants/international students as a cause of the housing crisis.

sources.title: Larissa Waters Facebook post on multicultural and migrant communities

malcolm-roberts-qld.summary: Roberts advocates cutting Australia's annual skilled and family migration intake to around 70,000 (a reduction of more than 60% from the 190,000 cap), describing this as 'zero net' migration, and has called for a parliamentary inquiry into the impacts of mass immigration on housing, wages, infrastructure and social cohesion.

sources.title: One Nation's Malcolm Roberts wants migration rate more than halved
Malcolm Roberts (politician)

jacinta-nampijinpa-price-nt.summary: On 3 September 2025 Price claimed the Labor government was giving preference to immigrants from particular countries, specifically India, citing concern over 'large numbers' arriving. She later retracted the claim that immigration law permits ethnic preference but said 'I don't believe I have anything to apologise about'; on 10 September 2025 Liberal leader Sussan Ley removed her from the shadow ministry after she refused to apologise.

sources.title: Jacinta Nampijinpa Price
Jacinta Nampijinpa Price's comments on migration continue to cause headaches for the Coalition

andrew-hastie-canning.summary: Andrew Hastie has stated that Australia's immigration numbers are 'too high, while the standards are too low,' arguing population growth has outpaced infrastructure and pressured housing and services and that 'the system needs a massive overhaul' (March 2026). In October 2025 he resigned from the Liberal shadow frontbench, saying he could not remain Shadow Home Affairs Minister 'and remain silent on immigration policy.'

sources.title: Andrew Hastie rallies the right on immigration levels at conservative conference in Perth
Andrew Hastie quits Liberal front bench over immigration row
Andrew Hastie

anne-aly-cowan.summary: As Minister for Multicultural Affairs, Aly stated in June 2025 that 'multiculturalism isn't solely about social cohesion and multicultural communities aren't solely responsible for social cohesion,' arguing that making such communities 'solely and wholly responsible for social cohesion' had added to a perception of them being 'over-securitised.' In 2016 she spoke against then-Immigration Minister Peter Dutton's comments regarding Lebanese Muslim immigration.

sources.title: Multicultural communities are not entirely responsible for social cohesion, says minister
Anne Aly

josh-wilson-fremantle.summary: In his September 2016 first speech, Josh Wilson said 'indefinite detention is wrong' and that 'the operation of the centres at Nauru and Manus Island has been unacceptable,' arguing that 'creating a properly constituted regional settlement framework is possible.'

sources.title: Josh Wilson (ALP-Fremantle) First Speech
First Speech: Mr Josh Wilson MP

kate-chaney-curtin.summary: Speaking on 26 March 2024 as the Migration Amendment (Removal and Other Measures) Bill 2024 was forced through the House, Kate Chaney said it was 'very very disappointing,' stating her community 'wants to see compassion when we are approaching asylum seekers.' She has also criticised the former 'fast track' processing system as flawed.

sources.title: Forcing the Migration Act Through The House - 26 March 2024
Speeches | Kate Chaney MP

zaneta-mascarenhas-swan.summary: In her first speech, Mascarenhas recounted that her Goan Indian father was told at the Australian embassy in Kenya 'You have the right skills, but you're the wrong colour' under the White Australia policy, and said her family's migration was only possible after the Whitlam government dismantled the last parts of that policy; she praised 'the most multicultural parliament we've had in history.'

sources.title: Indian-origin MP Zaneta Mascarenhas delivers inaugural speech
Goan origin MP Zaneta Mascarenhas delivers her first speech

david-pocock-act.summary: Pocock opposes offshore processing, calling for an end to it and a more human-rights-compliant refugee approach. After the government reauthorised Nauru offshore detention in February 2023 he called it a 'massive failure' and argued it 'makes no sense to be paying $420m of taxpayer money over three years to detain a small group of people.'

sources.title: David Pocock blasts Albanese government as it reauthorises Nauru offshore immigration detention
Immigration & Refugees

patrick-gorman-perth.summary: In his first speech to Parliament, Patrick Gorman said 'Perth welcomes new Australians and has a proud migrant history,' highlighting his electorate's multicultural and migrant heritage.

sources.title: First Speech — Patrick Gorman MP

sam-lim-tangney.summary: In his maiden speech (6 September 2022), Malaysian-born Sam Lim credited former Labor PM Gough Whitlam with ending the White Australia policy and introducing multiculturalism, saying 'because of him we can call Australia home.'

sources.title: Malaysian-born MP Sam Lim makes trilingual maiden speech
Australia's second Malaysian-born federal MP discusses the road ahead

katy-gallagher-act.summary: At a 2015 Palm Sunday refugee rally Gallagher expressed discomfort with offshore detention, telling Fairfax Media 'I am uncomfortable with what is going on now' and that few Australians, 'if they fully understood what was going on in detention centres and offshore processing, would be that comfortable with how it is being conducted.'

sources.title: ACT leads moves to soften ALP refugee policy
